title_original: Welinq and Pasqal Accelerate Networked Quantum Computing with Neutral-Atom Technology - Pasqal
summary_original: Pasqal and Welinq have deepened their strategic collaboration to accelerate the development of networked quantum computing using interconnected neutral-atom quantum processors. This new phase is marked by InterQo, a €4 million project funded by the Île-de-France Region and BPI France through the France 2030 initiative. InterQo links Pasqal, Welinq, and Alexei Ourjoumtsev’s group at Collège de France, focusing on integrating quantum networking capabilities—such as efficient photon extraction—directly into quantum processing units. Pasqal’s experience in quantum processor deployment and Welinq’s high-rate entanglement networking platform are being combined to create scalable, network-ready architectures, with the aim of overcoming current limits to scaling and supporting future data center integration.
